subject: LU-9906 osd: use pagevec for putting pages

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
values.+1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

messages.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

Just a quicky to speed up page cache operations on the server a bit.  Haven't benchmarked this, but it will be most beneficial under contention.  Shouldn't have much impact in the uncontended case.

Patch Set 1:

Looks good to me.

Patch Set 1:

Code-wise looks good to me.

Patch Set 1:

Oof, that build failure is a weird one.  SLES11SP4 is failing to build because of its own header file...?

I can try to dig around and find the SLES11SP4 source and see if I can figure how using pagevec is supposed to work.  Oof.  If anyone knows, help would be appreciated.

Patch Set 1:

Or, alternately, is there a SLES11SP4 source RPM squirreled away somewhere?  I'm not seeing one from/in Jenkins, but I might just be not finding it.

parents.subject: LU-10377 build: Update ZFS Version to 0.7.6

commit.subject: LU-9906 osd: use pagevec for putting pages
commit.message:
LU-9906 osd: use pagevec for putting pages

Using a pagevec instead of individual page puts is much
more efficient.  This should reduce contention on the page
cache allocation/freeing, which becomes a bottleneck with
high speed OSTs.

Cray-bug-id: LUS-5670
Signed-off-by: Patrick Farrell <paf@cray.com>
Change-Id: Ic15cb8e30887ec55e9348e50af307bfd7108c7e4
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/30531
Tested-by: Jenkins

Reviewed-by: Alexey Lyashkov <c17817@cray.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Dmitry Eremin <dmitry.eremin@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
